The Tides of Tomorrow

Coastal towns are rethinking how they live with the sea. Over the last decade, rising tides have reshaped harbours, footpaths, and the daily rhythm of fishing communities along the northern shore.

Researchers now track the waterline with cheap sensors and open data, turning what was once guesswork into a shared, public record that anyone can read and act on.

See also our follow-up report for details.
